title: Java Thread Lifecycle & Basic Multithreading Flow
description: A flowchart illustrating the lifecycle states of a Java thread and the basic flow of creating and managing threads using both Thread class and Runnable interface.
relationships: Thread/Runnable object created enters New state
Calling start() moves thread to Runnable state
JVM Scheduler selects a Runnable thread to enter Running state
Running thread can yield, sleep, or wait to enter Blocked/Waiting state
Blocked thread can be notified or timeout to return to Runnable state
run() method completes or thread stops to enter Terminated state
